title: quercus pilicaulis f. macrodonta
intro: Quercus pilicaulis f. macrodonta, also known as the Korean oak tree, is a subspecies of the Quercus pilicaulis tree, native to East Asia. The leaves and bark of this plant have been traditionally used in medicine for their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties. The medicinal properties of Quercus pilicaulis f. macrodonta are believed to provide health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating cough, and promoting digestive health. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas and infusions, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
